children.heading: Governor
children.label: 1 Governor
children.text: For a State to be eligible to receive an allotment under , the Governor of the State shall submit to the Secretary for consideration and approval, a single State plan (referred to in this subchapter as the “State plan”) that outlines a 4-year strategy for the statewide provision of community service employment and other authorized activities for eligible individuals under this subchapter. The plan shall contain such provisions as the Secretary may require, consistent with this subchapter, including a description of the process used to ensure the participation of individuals described in paragraph (2). Not less often than every 2 years, the Governor shall review the State plan and submit an update to the State plan to the Secretary for consideration and approval.section 3056d of this title

children.label: A
children.text: individuals representing the State agency and the area agencies on aging in the State, and the State and local workforce development boards established under title I of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act [ et seq.];29 U.S.C. 3111

children.label: B
children.text: individuals representing public and nonprofit private agencies and organizations providing employment services, including each grantee operating a project under this subchapter in the State; and

children.label: C
children.text: individuals representing social service organizations providing services to older individuals, grantees under subchapter III of this chapter, affected communities, unemployed older individuals, community-based organizations serving the needs of older individuals, business organizations, and labor organizations.

children.heading: Comments
children.label: 3 Comments
children.text: Any State plan submitted by the Governor in accordance with paragraph (1) shall be accompanied by copies of public comments relating to the plan received pursuant to paragraph (8), and a summary of the comments.

children.label: A
children.text: the relationship that the number of eligible individuals in each area bears to the total number of eligible individuals, respectively, in the State;

children.label: B
children.text: the relative distribution of eligible individuals residing in rural and urban areas in the State; and

children.label: i
children.text: eligible individuals who are individuals with greatest economic need;

children.label: ii
children.text: eligible individuals who are minority individuals;

children.label: iii
children.text: eligible individuals who are limited English proficient;

children.label: iv
children.text: eligible individuals who are individuals with greatest social need; and

children.label: v
children.text: eligible individuals who have been incarcerated within the last 5 years or are under supervision following release from prison or jail within the last 5 years;

children.label: D
children.text: the current and projected employment opportunities in the State (such as by providing information available under section 49–2 of title 29 by occupation), and the type of skills possessed by local eligible individuals;l

children.label: E
children.text: the localities and populations for which projects of the type authorized by this subchapter are most needed; and

children.label: F
children.text: how the activities of grantees in the State under this subchapter will be coordinated with activities carried out in the State under title I of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act ( et seq.) and other related programs (referred to in this subparagraph as “WIOA and related activities”), and how the State will reduce unnecessary duplication between the activities carried out under this subchapter and the WIOA and related activities.29 U.S.C. 3111

children.label: A
children.text: recommendations regarding the anticipated effect of each such proposal upon the overall distribution of enrollment positions under this subchapter in the State (including such distribution among urban and rural areas), taking into account the total number of positions to be provided by all grantees in the State;

children.label: B
children.text: any recommendations for redistribution of positions to underserved areas as vacancies occur in previously encumbered positions in other areas; and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of any increase in funding that may be available for use in the State under this subchapter for the fiscal year, any recommendations for distribution of newly available positions in excess of those available during the preceding year to underserved areas.

children.heading: Combined State plan
children.label: 6 Combined State plan
children.text: In lieu of the plan described in paragraph (1), a State may develop and submit a combined State plan in accordance with section 103 of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (). For a State that obtains approval of such a combined State plan, that section 103 shall apply in lieu of this subsection and a reference in any other provision of this subchapter (other than this subsection) to a State plan shall be considered to be a reference to that combined State plan.29 U.S.C. 3113

children.heading: Disruptions
children.label: 7 Disruptions
children.text: In developing a plan or considering a recommendation under this subsection, the Governor shall avoid disruptions in the provision of services for participants to the greatest possible extent.

children.heading: Determination
children.label: A Determination
children.text: In order to effectively carry out this subchapter, each State shall make the State plan available for public comment. The Secretary, in consultation with the Assistant Secretary, shall review the plan and make a written determination with findings and a decision regarding the plan.

children.heading: Review
children.label: B Review
children.text: The Secretary may review, on the Secretary’s own initiative or at the request of any public or private agency or organization or of any agency of the State, the distribution of projects and services under this subchapter in the State, including the distribution between urban and rural areas in the State. For each proposed reallocation of projects or services in a State, the Secretary shall give notice and opportunity for public comment.

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: 9 Exemption
children.text: The grantees that serve eligible individuals who are older Indians or Pacific Island and Asian Americans with funds reserved under may not be required to participate in the State planning processes described in this section but shall collaborate with the Secretary to develop a plan for projects and services to eligible individuals who are Indians or Pacific Island and Asian Americans, respectively.section 3056d(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Coordination with other Federal programs
children.label: b Coordination with other Federal programs

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary and the Assistant Secretary shall coordinate the program carried out under this subchapter with programs carried out under other subchapters of this chapter, to increase employment opportunities available to older individuals.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall coordinate programs carried out under this subchapter with the program carried out under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act, the Community Services Block Grant Act ( et seq.), the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ( et seq.), the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 ( et seq.), the National and Community Service Act of 1990 ( et seq.), and the Domestic Volunteer Service Act of 1973 ( et seq.). The Secretary shall coordinate the administration of this subchapter with the administration of other subchapters of this chapter by the Assistant Secretary to increase the likelihood that eligible individuals for whom employment opportunities under this subchapter are available and who need services under such subchapters receive such services.42 U.S.C. 990129 U.S.C. 70120 U.S.C. 230142 U.S.C. 1250142 U.S.C. 4950

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: i Prohibition
children.text: Funds appropriated to carry out this subchapter may not be used to carry out any program under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act, the Community Services Block Grant Act, the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006, the National and Community Service Act of 1990, or the Domestic Volunteer Service Act of 1973.

children.heading: Joint activities
children.label: ii Joint activities
children.text: Clause (i) shall not be construed to prohibit carrying out projects under this subchapter jointly with programs, projects, or activities under any Act specified in clause (i), or from carrying out .section 3056i of this title

children.heading: Informational materials on age discrimination
children.label: 3 Informational materials on age discrimination
children.text: The Secretary shall distribute to grantees under this subchapter, for distribution to program participants, and at no cost to grantees or participants, informational materials developed and supplied by the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission and other appropriate Federal agencies that the Secretary determines are designed to help participants identify age discrimination and to understand their rights under the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 ( et seq.).29 U.S.C. 621

children.heading: Use of services, equipment, personnel, and facilities
children.label: c Use of services, equipment, personnel, and facilities
children.text: In carrying out this subchapter, the Secretary may use the services, equipment, personnel, and facilities of Federal and other agencies, with their consent, with or without reimbursement, and on a similar basis cooperate with other public and nonprofit private agencies and organizations in the use of services, equipment, and facilities.

children.heading: Payments
children.label: d Payments
children.text: Payments under this subchapter may be made in advance or by way of reimbursement and in such installments as the Secretary may determine.

children.heading: No delegation of functions
children.label: e No delegation of functions
children.text: The Secretary shall not delegate any function of the Secretary under this subchapter to any other Federal officer or entity.

children.heading: Monitoring
children.label: 1 Monitoring
children.text: The Secretary shall monitor projects for which grants are made under this subchapter to determine whether the grantees are complying with rules and regulations issued to carry out this subchapter (including the statewide planning, consultation, and coordination requirements of this subchapter).

children.heading: Compliance with uniform cost principles and administrative requirements
children.label: 2 Compliance with uniform cost principles and administrative requirements
children.text: Each grantee that receives funds under this subchapter shall comply with the applicable uniform cost principles and appropriate administrative requirements for grants and contracts that are applicable to the type of entity that receives funds, as issued as circulars or rules of the Office of Management and Budget.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 3 Reports
children.text: Each grantee described in paragraph (2) shall prepare and submit a report in such manner and containing such information as the Secretary may require regarding activities carried out under this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: are sufficient to permit the preparation of reports required by this subchapter;

children.label: B
children.text: are sufficient to permit the tracing of funds to a level of expenditure adequate to ensure that the funds have not been spent unlawfully; and

children.label: C
children.text: contain any other information that the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Evaluations
children.label: g Evaluations
children.text: The Secretary shall establish by rule and implement a process to evaluate, in accordance with , the performance of projects carried out and services provided under this subchapter. The Secretary shall report to Congress, and make available to the public, the results of each such evaluation and shall use such evaluation to improve services delivered by, or the operation of, projects carried out under this subchapter.section 3056k of this title
